completed.summary: Inspected OS, CPU, RAM, GPU, disk, Python, Git, Node.js, Java, and compilers.
Created EviCode directory structure and reproducibility skeleton.
Installed Python, Git, Node.js, MiKTeX, and project-local JDK 17.
Ran unit tests, synthetic smoke pipeline, verification report generation, and paper build.
Added VerificationReport and reframed the paper around evidence contribution and explanation.
Built 2,952 directed HumanEval-X verification examples across Python, Java, and JavaScript.
Extracted static and execution evidence, then refreshed static evidence with tree-sitter parsers.
Ran grouped evidence-fusion experiments, bootstrap F1 intervals, and exact McNemar comparisons.
Generated benchmark tables, figures, and paper/output/paper.pdf from the single-file manuscript.
Refocused EviCode around evidence taxonomy, informativeness, complementarity, cost, budget status, and failure explanations.
Added token, edit, AST shape, control-flow, operator, API/call, identifier-role, data-flow, and type evidence features.
Generated analysis CSV/JSON outputs plus paper tables and figures for evidence-study research questions.
Added metric comparison, evidence hierarchy, attribution, language analysis, failure detection coverage, Pareto analysis, guidelines, and self-review outputs.
Evaluated 7,312 Python-to-Java LLM translations from DeepSeekCoder, QwenCoder, and StarCoder for continuous verification confidence and calibration.

Created five-reviewer critique and rewrote the single-file manuscript around semantic verification, evidence formalization, running examples, expanded related work, interpretation, and threats to validity.

Created the Phase IV five-reviewer report, rewrote the manuscript around verification motivation, metric evidence history, execution availability, richer examples, expanded formal definitions, stronger evidence-source explanations, and a deeper discussion; rebuilt paper/output/paper.pdf.

Added conceptual evidence-ladder figure, interpretive surprising-findings table, qualitative disagreement cases, expanded discussion of why metrics behave as observed, revised title, and strengthened conclusion.
